chief_complaint: A 3-year-old, female neutered Bichon Frise presented with a 2-day history of behavioral changes and ataxia.
history: The patient was presented with a 2-day history of behavioral changes and ataxia. The neurological examination was consistent with diffuse forebrain involvement, with left lateralization.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings: diffuse forebrain involvement, left lateralization.
labs: MRI study: bilaterally symmetrical T2W/T2 fluid-attenuated inversion recovery (T2-FLAIR) hyperintense, T1W hypointense, non-enhancing lesions involving the basal nuclei, with restricted diffusion and hemorrhage affecting both caudate nuclei. Similar bilaterally symmetrical T2W/T2-FLAIR hyperintense lesions were noted in the hypothalamus, midbrain, and occipital lobes. Histopathological examination indicated concomitant granulomatous meningoencephalitis and necrotizing encephalitis.
correct_diagnosis: Concomitant granulomatous meningoencephalitis and necrotizing encephalitis
differentials: Concomitant granulomatous meningoencephalitis and necrotizing encephalitis
correct_ranking: Concomitant granulomatous meningoencephalitis and necrotizing encephalitis
